qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Collaborator-visible dashboard config exposes the Parse master key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authenticated secret disclosure in GET /parse-dashboard-config.json and related frontend rendering, while the advisory is missing CSRF protection on POST /apps/:appId/agent. The endpoint, missing guard, and exploit mechanism are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing CSRF protection on the agent endpoint.
evidence: The advisory describes a CSRF flaw on POST /apps/:appId/agent where a malicious page can submit requests through an authenticated victim session; the documented fix is CSRF middleware plus a dashboard CSRF token.
The only qualifying finding is rooted in GET /parse-dashboard-config.json and frontend handling that exposes an app's raw masterKey to an already authenticated collaborator.

The session's separate agent-related finding is non-qualifying and describes missing authentication when dashboard users are unset, which is a different root cause from the advisory's missing CSRF protection for authenticated sessions.
